Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Nominal clause with existential marker 'atin' + possessed noun phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: There is a hidden motive or additional story behind what is being said
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: May nakatagong dahilan o dagdag na kuwento
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from rural storytelling traditions where news often carried unseen implications
First Example Sentence: E ku maniwala keng sinabi na; atin yang buntut ing balita.
Second Example Sentence: Nung akakit mu ing nangyari, malalam mu atin yang buntut ing balita.
Third Example Sentence: Ali mu agad paniwalan, kasi atin yang buntut ing balita.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Predicate adjective phrase describing stubborn behavior
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone extremely stubborn or unwilling to change opinion
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Stone imagery symbolizes immovable firmness in Kapampangan oral metaphors
First Example Sentence: Ing kapatad ku mikatuliran ya king batu; ali ya papatalo.
Second Example Sentence: Maski mipakit mu na ing ebidensiya, mikatuliran ya king batu.
Third Example Sentence: Ali mu na pilitan, mikatuliran ya king batu talaga.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone with strong courage or bold personality
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional Kapampangan praise for warriors and brave individuals
First Example Sentence: Mabagsik ya ing lub na ning lalaki; ali ya matakut.
Second Example Sentence: King laban, mabagsik ya ing lub na ning kapitan.
Third Example Sentence: Dakal yang problema pero mabagsik ya ing lub.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Something pleasant or beautiful to look at
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common aesthetic expression in Kapampangan descriptions of landscapes
First Example Sentence: Makalugud ya ing mata ning banua king abak.
Second Example Sentence: Ing hardin da makalugud ya ing mata.
Third Example Sentence: Ing disenyu na makalugud ya ing mata.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Reflexive verb phrase with locative complement
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Metaphor referencing futile struggles against nature
First Example Sentence: Ing argumento mu mipaglaban ya king hangin.
Second Example Sentence: Ali mu na ipilit, mipaglaban ya king hangin.
Third Example Sentence: Ing reklamu da mipaglaban ya king hangin.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To humble someone who is arrogant
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from gesture of pressing someone's head downward
First Example Sentence: Ing kapitan tuknangan ya ing ulun ning mayabang.
Second Example Sentence: Kailangan mu tuknangan ya ing ulun.
Third Example Sentence: King debate tuknangan ya ing ulun.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone who speaks sharply or critically
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Metaphor comparing speech to a sharp blade
First Example Sentence: Masalese ya ing dila ning ale ku.
Second Example Sentence: Ali ku buri kaku ya masalese ya ing dila.
Third Example Sentence: Ing komentar na masalese ya ing dila.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional expression indicating repetition over many days
First Example Sentence: Mekapilan ya king aldo ing problema na.
Second Example Sentence: Mekapilan ya king aldo yang dumalan.
Third Example Sentence: Mekapilan ya king aldo ing balita.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A situation that has escalated greatly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Water imagery frequently used in Kapampangan metaphors for escalation
First Example Sentence: Ing problema me kadakal yang danum.
Second Example Sentence: Mekadakal yang danum ing alitan da.
Third Example Sentence: Ali na kontroladu, mekadakal yang danum.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To proceed without knowledge or certainty
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: Kumikilos nang walang sapat na impormasyon
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Darkness metaphor widely used in Kapampangan narratives to indicate uncertainty
First Example Sentence: Ing lider mipaglakad ya king dilim nung ali ya magplanu.
Second Example Sentence: Mipaglakad ya king dilim ing proyekto nung ala yang datos.
Third Example Sentence: Ali ku buri mipaglakad ya king dilim.
